}\n\n    constructor(props: IProps) {\n        super(props, HeaderKind.Room);\n\n        if (this.props.room && !this.supportsThreadNotifications) {\n            this.threadNotificationState = RoomNotificationStateStore.instance.globalState;\n    }\n\n    public componentDidMount(): void {\n        super.componentDidMount();\n        if (!this.supportsThreadNotifications) {\n useState<Set<string>>(new Set());\n68: \n69:     const update = useCallback((ev?: MatrixEvent) => {\n70:         if (!room) return;\n71:         if (ev && ev.getType() !== ReadPinsEventId) useTypedEventEmitter(room, RoomEvent.AccountData, update);\n77:     useEffect(() => {\n78:         update();\n79:         return () => {\n80:             setReadPinnedEvents(new Set());\n81:         };\n82:     }, [update]);\n83:     return readPinnedEvents;\n84: };\n85: \n86: const PinnedMessagesCard = ({ room, onClose, permalinkCreator }: IProps) => {\n87:     const cli = = RightPanelStore.instance;\n        if (rps.currentCard.phase == phase && !cardState && rps.isOpen) {\n            rps.togglePanel(null);\n        } else {\n            RightPanelStore.instance.setCard({ phase, state: cardState });\n            if (!rps.isOpen) rps.togglePanel(null);\n        }\n    }\n\n    public isPhase(phases: string | string[]): boolean {\n this.state.phase;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private onRightPanelStoreUpdate = () => {\n        if (this.unmounted) return;\n        this.setState({ phase: RightPanelStore.instance.currentCard.phase });\n    };\n\n    // XXX: Make renderButtons a prop\n    public abstract renderButtons(): JSX.Element;\n\n    public render() {\n        return <div className=\"mx_HeaderButtons\" role=\"tablist\">\n \"matrix-js-sdk/src/models/event\";\nimport { EventType, RelationType } from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/@types/event';\nimport { logger } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/logger\";\nimport { RoomStateEvent } from \"../../../../res/img/element-icons/room/message-bar/emoji.svg\";\nimport { Icon as ReplyIcon } from '../../../../res/img/element-icons/room/message-bar/reply.svg';\nimport { _t } from \"../../../languageHandler\";\nimport BaseCard from \"./BaseCard\";\nimport Spinner from \"../elements/Spinner\";\nimport MatrixClientContext from \"../../../contexts/MatrixClientContext\";\nimport { useTypedEventEmitter } from \"../../../hooks/useEventEmitter\";\nimport PinningUtils from \"../../../utils/PinningUtils\";\nimport { useAsyncMemo } from \"../../../hooks/useAsyncMemo\";\nimport PinnedEventTile from \"../rooms/PinnedEventTile\";\nimport { useRoomState } from \"../../../hooks/useRoomState\";\nimport RoomContext, { TimelineRenderingType } from \"../../../contexts/RoomContext\";\nimport { ReadPinsEventId } from \"./types\";\nimport Heading from '../typography/Heading';\nimport { RoomPermalinkCreator } from \"../../../utils/permalinks/Permalinks\";\n\ninterface IProps {\n    room: Room;\n    permalinkCreator: RoomPermalinkCreator;\n    onClose(): void;\n}\n\nexport const usePinnedEvents = (room: Room): string[] => {\n    const [pinnedEvents, setPinnedEvents] = useState<string[]>([]);\n\n    const update = useCallback((ev?: MatrixEvent) => {\n        if (!room) return;\n        if (ev && ev.getType() !== EventType.RoomPinnedEvents) return;\n        setPinnedEvents(room.currentState.getStateEvents(EventType.RoomPinnedEvents, \"\")?.getContent()?.pinned || []);\n    }, [room]);\n\n    useTypedEventEmitter(room?.currentState, RoomStateEvent.Events, update);\n    useEffect(() => {\n        update();\n        return () => {\n            setPinnedEvents([]);\n        };\n    }, [update]);\n    return pinnedEvents;\n};\n\nexport const useReadPinnedEvents = (room: Room): Set<string> => {\n    const [readPinnedEvents, setReadPinnedEvents] = useState<Set<string>>(new Set());\n\n    const update = useCallback((ev?: MatrixEvent) => {\n        if (!room) return;\n        if (ev && ev.getType() !== ReadPinsEventId) return;\n        const readPins = room.getAccountData(ReadPinsEventId)?.getContent()?.event_ids;\n        setReadPinnedEvents(new Set(readPins || []));\n    }, [room]);\n\n    useTypedEventEmitter(room, RoomEvent.AccountData, update);\n    useEffect(() => {\n        update();\n        return () => {\n            setReadPinnedEvents(new Set());\n        };\n    }, [update]);\n    return readPinnedEvents;\n};\n\nconst PinnedMessagesCard = ({ room, onClose, permalinkCreator }: IProps) => {\n    const cli = useContext(MatrixClientContext);\n    const roomContext = useContext(RoomContext);\n    const canUnpin = useRoomState(room, state => state.mayClientSendStateEvent(EventType.RoomPinnedEvents, cli));\n    const pinnedEventIds = usePinnedEvents(room);\n    const readPinnedEvents = useReadPinnedEvents(room);\n\n    useEffect(() => {\n        const newlyRead = pinnedEventIds.filter(id => !readPinnedEvents.has(id));\n        if (newlyRead.length > 0) {\n            // clear out any read pinned events which no longer are pinned\n            cli.setRoomAccountData(room.roomId, ReadPinsEventId, {\n                event_ids: pinnedEventIds,\n            });\n        }\n    }, [cli, room.roomId, pinnedEventIds, readPinnedEvents]);\n\n    const pinnedEvents = useAsyncMemo(() => {\n        const promises = pinnedEventIds.map(async eventId => {\n            const timelineSet = room.getUnfilteredTimelineSet();\n            const localEvent = timelineSet?.getTimelineForEvent(eventId)?.getEvents().find(e => e.getId() === eventId);\n            if (localEvent) return PinningUtils.isPinnable(localEvent) ? localEvent : null;\n\n            try {\n                // Fetch the event and latest edit in parallel\n                const [evJson, { events: [edit] }] = await Promise.all([\n                    cli.fetchRoomEvent(room.roomId, eventId),\n                    cli.relations(room.roomId, eventId, RelationType.Replace, null, { limit: 1 }),\n                ]);\n                const event = new MatrixEvent(evJson);\n                if (event.isEncrypted()) {\n                    await cli.decryptEventIfNeeded(event); // TODO await?\n                }\n\n                if (event && PinningUtils.isPinnable(event)) {\n                    // Inject sender information\n                    event.sender = room.getMember(event.getSender());\n                    // Also inject any edits we've found\n                    if (edit) event.makeReplaced(edit);\n\n                    return event;\n                }\n            } catch (err) {\n                logger.error(\"Error looking up pinned event \" + eventId + \" in room \" + room.roomId);\n                logger.error(err);\n            }\n            return null;\n        });\n\n        return Promise.all(promises);\n    }, [cli, room, pinnedEventIds], null);\n\n    let content;\n    if (!pinnedEvents) {\n        content = <Spinner />;\n    } else if (pinnedEvents.length > 0) {\n        const onUnpinClicked = async (event: MatrixEvent) => {\n            const pinnedEvents = room.currentState.getStateEvents(EventType.RoomPinnedEvents, \"\");\n            if (pinnedEvents?.getContent()?.pinned) {\n                const pinned = pinnedEvents.getContent().pinned;\n                const index = pinned.indexOf(event.getId());\n                if (index !== -1) {\n                    pinned.splice(index, 1);\n                    await cli.sendStateEvent(room.roomId, EventType.RoomPinnedEvents, { pinned }, \"\");\n                }\n            }\n        };\n\n        // show them in reverse, with latest pinned at the top\n        content = pinnedEvents.filter(Boolean).reverse().map(ev => (\n            <PinnedEventTile\n                key={ev.getId()}\n                event={ev}\n                onUnpinClicked={canUnpin ? () => onUnpinClicked(ev) : undefined}\n                permalinkCreator={permalinkCreator}\n            />\n        ));\n    } else {\n        content = <div className=\"mx_PinnedMessagesCard_empty_wrapper\">\n            <div className=\"mx_PinnedMessagesCard_empty\">\n                { /* XXX: We reuse the classes for simplicity, but deliberately not the components for non-interactivity. */ }\n                <div className=\"mx_MessageActionBar mx_PinnedMessagesCard_MessageActionBar\">\n                    <div className=\"mx_MessageActionBar_iconButton\">\n                        <EmojiIcon />\n                    </div>\n                    <div className=\"mx_MessageActionBar_iconButton\">\n                        <ReplyIcon />\n                    </div>\n                    <div className=\"mx_MessageActionBar_iconButton mx_MessageActionBar_optionsButton\">\n                        <ContextMenuIcon />\n                    </div>\n                </div>\n\n                <Heading size=\"h4\" className=\"mx_PinnedMessagesCard_empty_header\">{ _t(\"Nothing pinned, yet\") }</Heading>\n                { _t(\"If you have permissions, open the menu on any message and select \" +\n                    \"<b>Pin</b> to stick them here.\", {}, {\n                    b: sub => <b>{ sub }</b>,\n                }) }\n            </div>\n        </div>;\n    }\n\n    return <BaseCard\n        header={<div className=\"mx_BaseCard_header_title\">\n            <Heading size=\"h4\" className=\"mx_BaseCard_header_title_heading\">{ _t(\"Pinned messages\") }</Heading>\n        </div>}\n        className=\"mx_PinnedMessagesCard\"\n        onClose={onClose}\n    >\n        <RoomContext.Provider value={{\n            ...roomContext,\n            timelineRenderingType: TimelineRenderingType.Pinned,\n        }}>\n            { content }\n        </RoomContext.Provider>\n    </BaseCard>;\n};\n\nexport default PinnedMessagesCard;\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1136,"cache_read_input_tokens":47191,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1136,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":18,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"764b2d1a-e591-4044-b160-ca69453679bf","uuid":"baa45493-634b-48aa-9fe8-325684f0226c"}

E.g.\\n273:  * { 'Hello (.?) World': (sub) => sub.toUpperCase() }\\n274:  *\\n275:  * @return a React <span> component if any non-strings were used in substitutions, otherwise a string\\n276:  */\\n277: export function replaceByRegexes(text: string, mapping: IVariables): string;\\n278: export function replaceByRegexes(text: string, mapping: Tags): React.ReactNode;\\n279: export function replaceByRegexes(text: string, mapping: IVariables | Tags): string | React.ReactNode {\\n280:     // We initially store our output as an array of strings and objects (e.g. React components).\\n281:     // This will then be converted to a string or a <span> at the end\\n282:     const output = [text];\\n283: \\n284:     // If we insert any components we need to wrap the output in a span. React doesn't like just an array of components.\\n285:     let shouldWrapInSpan = false;\\n286: \\n287:     for (const regexpString in mapping) {\\n288:         // TODO: Cache regexps\\n289:         const regexp = new RegExp(regexpString, \\\"g\\\");\\n290: \\n291:         // Loop over what output we have so far and perform replacements\\n292:         // We look for matches: if we find one, we get three parts: everything before the match, the replaced part,\\n293:         // and everything after the match. Insert all three into the output. We need to do this because we can insert objects.\\n294:         // Otherwise there would be no need for the splitting and we could do simple replacement.\\n295:         let matchFoundSomewhere = false; // If we don't find a match anywhere we want to log it\\n296:         for (let outputIndex = 0; outputIndex < output.length; outputIndex++) {\\n297:             const inputText = output[outputIndex];\\n298:             if (typeof inputText !== 'string') { // We might have inserted objects earlier, don't try to replace them\\n299:                 continue;\\n300:             }\\n301: \\n302:             // process every match in the string\\n303:             // starting with the first\\n304:             let match = regexp.exec(inputText);\\n305: \\n306:             if (!match) continue;\\n307:             matchFoundSomewhere = true;\\n308: \\n309:             // The textual part before the first match\\n310:             const head = inputText.slice(0, match.index);\\n311: \\n312:             const parts = [];\\n313:             // keep track of prevMatch\\n314:             let prevMatch;\\n315:             while (match) {\\n316:                 // store prevMatch\\n317:                 prevMatch = match;\\n318:                 const capturedGroups = match.slice(2);\\n319: \\n320:                 let replaced;\\n321:                 // If substitution is a function, call it\\n322:                 if (mapping[regexpString] instanceof Function) {\\n323:                     replaced = ((mapping as Tags)[regexpString] as Function)(...capturedGroups);\\n324:                 } else {\\n325:                     replaced = mapping[regexpString];\\n326:                 }\\n327: \\n328:                 if (typeof replaced === 'object') {\\n329:                     shouldWrapInSpan = true;\\n330:                 }\\n331: \\n332:                 // Here we also need to check that it actually is a string before comparing against one\\n333:                 // The head and tail are always strings\\n334:                 if (typeof replaced !== 'string' || replaced !== '') {\\n335:                     parts.push(replaced);\\n336:                 }\\n337: \\n338:                 // try the next match\\n339:                 match = regexp.exec(inputText);\\n340: \\n341:                 // add the text between prevMatch and this one\\n342:                 // or the end of the string if prevMatch is the last match\\n343:                 let tail;\\n344:                 if (match) {\\n345:                     const startIndex = prevMatch.index + prevMatch[0].length;\\n346:                     tail = inputText.slice(startIndex, match.index);\\n347:                 } else {\\n348:                     tail = inputText.slice(prevMatch.index + prevMatch[0].length);\\n349:                 }\\n350:                 if (tail) {\\n351:                     parts.push(tail);\\n352:                 }\\n353:             }\\n354: \\n355:             // Insert in reverse order as splice does insert-before and this way we get the final order correct\\n356:             // remove the old element at the same time\\n357:             output.splice(outputIndex, 1, ...parts);\\n358: \\n359:             if (head !== '') { // Don't push empty nodes, they are of no use\\n360:                 output.splice(outputIndex, 0, head);\\n361:             }\\n362:         }\\n363:         if (!matchFoundSomewhere) { // The current regexp did not match anything in the input\\n364:             // Missing matches is entirely possible because you might choose to show some variables only in the case\\n365:             // of e.g. plurals. It's still a bit suspicious, and could be due to an error, so log it.\\n366:             // However, not showing count is so common that it's not worth logging. And other commonly unused variables\\n367:             // here, if there are any.\\n368:             if (regexpString !== '%\\\\\\\\(count\\\\\\\\)s') {\\n369:                 logger.log(`Could not E.g.\\n273:  * { 'Hello (.?) World': (sub) => sub.toUpperCase() }\\n274:  *\\n275:  * @return a React <span> component if any non-strings were used in substitutions, otherwise a string\\n276:  */\\n277: export function replaceByRegexes(text: string, mapping: IVariables): string;\\n278: export function replaceByRegexes(text: string, mapping: Tags): React.ReactNode;\\n279: export function replaceByRegexes(text: string, mapping: IVariables | Tags): string | React.ReactNode {\\n280:     // We initially store our output as an array of strings and objects (e.g. React components).\\n281:     // This will then be converted to a string or a <span> at the end\\n282:     const output = [text];\\n283: \\n284:     // If we insert any components we need to wrap the output in a span. React doesn't like just an array of components.\\n285:     let shouldWrapInSpan = false;\\n286: \\n287:     for (const regexpString in mapping) {\\n288:         // TODO: Cache regexps\\n289:         const regexp = new RegExp(regexpString, \\\"g\\\");\\n290: \\n291:         // Loop over what output we have so far and perform replacements\\n292:         // We look for matches: if we find one, we get three parts: everything before the match, the replaced part,\\n293:         // and everything after the match. Insert all three into the output. We need to do this because we can insert objects.\\n294:         // Otherwise there would be no need for the splitting and we could do simple replacement.\\n295:         let matchFoundSomewhere = false; // If we don't find a match anywhere we want to log it\\n296:         for (let outputIndex = 0; outputIndex < output.length; outputIndex++) {\\n297:             const inputText = output[outputIndex];\\n298:             if (typeof inputText !== 'string') { // We might have inserted objects earlier, don't try to replace them\\n299:                 continue;\\n300:             }\\n301: \\n302:             // process every match in the string\\n303:             // starting with the first\\n304:             let match = regexp.exec(inputText);\\n305: \\n306:             if (!match) continue;\\n307:             matchFoundSomewhere = true;\\n308: \\n309:             // The textual part before the first match\\n310:             const head = inputText.slice(0, match.index);\\n311: \\n312:             const parts = [];\\n313:             // keep track of prevMatch\\n314:             let prevMatch;\\n315:             while (match) {\\n316:                 // store prevMatch\\n317:                 prevMatch = match;\\n318:                 const capturedGroups = match.slice(2);\\n319: \\n320:                 let replaced;\\n321:                 // If substitution is a function, call it\\n322:                 if (mapping[regexpString] instanceof Function) {\\n323:                     replaced = ((mapping as Tags)[regexpString] as Function)(...capturedGroups);\\n324:                 } else {\\n325:                     replaced = mapping[regexpString];\\n326:                 }\\n327: \\n328:                 if (typeof replaced === 'object') {\\n329:                     shouldWrapInSpan = true;\\n330:                 }\\n331: \\n332:                 // Here we also need to check that it actually is a string before comparing against one\\n333:                 // The head and tail are always strings\\n334:                 if (typeof replaced !== 'string' || replaced !== '') {\\n335:                     parts.push(replaced);\\n336:                 }\\n337: \\n338:                 // try the next match\\n339:                 match = regexp.exec(inputText);\\n340: \\n341:                 // add the text between prevMatch and this one\\n342:                 // or the end of the string if prevMatch is the last match\\n343:                 let tail;\\n344:                 if (match) {\\n345:                     const startIndex = prevMatch.index + prevMatch[0].length;\\n346:                     tail = inputText.slice(startIndex, match.index);\\n347:                 } else {\\n348:                     tail = inputText.slice(prevMatch.index + prevMatch[0].length);\\n349:                 }\\n350:                 if (tail) {\\n351:                     parts.push(tail);\\n352:                 }\\n353:             }\\n354: \\n355:             // Insert in reverse order as splice does insert-before and this way we get the final order correct\\n356:             // remove the old element at the same time\\n357:             output.splice(outputIndex, 1, ...parts);\\n358: \\n359:             if (head !== '') { // Don't push empty nodes, they are of no use\\n360:                 output.splice(outputIndex, 0, head);\\n361:             }\\n362:         }\\n363:         if (!matchFoundSomewhere) { // The current regexp did not match anything in the input\\n364:             // Missing matches is entirely possible because you might choose to show some variables only in the case\\n365:             // of e.g. plurals. It's still a bit suspicious, and could be due to an error, so log it.\\n366:             // However, not showing count is so common that it's not worth logging. And other commonly unused variables\\n367:             // here, if there are any.\\n368:             if (regexpString !== '%\\\\\\\\(count\\\\\\\\)s') {\\n369:                 logger.log(`Could not find ${regexp} in ${text}`);\\n370:             }\\n371:         }\\n372:     }\\n373: \\n374:     if (shouldWrapInSpan) {\\n375:         return React.createElement('span', null, ...output);\\n376: }\\n387: \\n388: type Languages = {\\n389:     [lang: string]: {\\n390:         fileName: string;\\n391:         label: string;\\n392:     };\\n393: };\\n394: \\n395: export function The string isn't in the translations dictionary, usually because you're in develop\n98:             // and haven't run yarn i18n\n99:             // 2. Loading the translation resources over the network failed, which can happen due to\n100:             // to network or if the client tried to load a translation that's been removed from the\n101:             // server.\n102:             //\n103:             // At this point, its the lesser evil to show the untranslated text, which\n104:             // will be in English, so the user can still make out *something*, rather than an opaque\n105:             // \"missing translation\" error.\n106:             //\n107:             // Don't do this in develop so people remember to run yarn i18n.\n108:             return { translated: text, isFallback: true };\n109:         }\n110:         return { translated: fallbackTranslated, isFallback: true };\n111:     }\n112:     return { translated };\n113: };\n114: \n115: // Wrapper for counterpart's translation function so that it handles nulls and undefineds properly\n116: // Takes the same arguments as counterpart.translate()\n117: function safeCounterpartTranslate(text: string, variables?: object) {\n118:     // Don't do substitutions in counterpart. We handle it ourselves so we can replace with React components\n119:     // However, still pass the variables to counterpart so that it can choose the correct plural if count is given\n120:     // It is enough to pass the count variable, but in the future counterpart might make use of other information too\n121:     const options = { ...variables, interpolate: false };\n122: \n123:     // Horrible hack to avoid https://github.com/vector-im/element-web/issues/4191\n124:     // The interpolation library that counterpart uses does not support undefined/null\n125:     // values and instead will throw an error. This is a problem since everywhere else\n126:     // in JS land passing undefined/null will simply stringify instead, and when converting\n127:     // valid ES6 template strings to i18n strings it's extremely easy to pass undefined/null\n128:     // if there are no existing null guards. To avoid this making the app completely inoperable,\n129:     // we'll check all the values for undefined/null and stringify them here.\n130:     if (options && typeof options === 'object') {\n131:         Object.keys(options).forEach((k) => {\n132:             if (options[k] === undefined) {\n133:                 logger.warn(\"safeCounterpartTranslate called with undefined interpolation name: \" + k);\n134:                 options[k] = 'undefined';\n135:             }\n136:             if (options[k] === null) {\n137:                 logger.warn(\"safeCounterpartTranslate called with null interpolation name: \" + k);\n138:                 options[k] = 'null';\n139:             }\n140:         });\n141:     }\n142:     return translateWithFallback(text, options);\n143: }\n144: \n145: /*\n171:  * Translates text and optionally also replaces XML-ish elements in the text with e.g. React components\n172:  * @param {string} text The untranslated text, e.g \"click <a>here</a> now to %(foo)s\".\n173:  * @param {object} variables Variable substitutions, e.g { foo: 'bar' }\n174:  * @param {object} tags Tag substitutions e.g. { 'a': (sub) => <a>{sub}</a> }\n175:  *\n176:  * In both variables and tags, the values to substitute with can be either simple strings, React components,\n177:  * or functions that return the value to use in the substitution (e.g. return a React component). In case of\n178:  * a tag replacement, the function receives as the argument the text inside the element corresponding to the tag.\n179:  *\n180:  * Use tag substitutions if you need to translate text between tags (e.g. \"<a>Click here!</a>\"), otherwise\n181:  * you will end up with literal \"<a>\" in your output, rather than HTML. Note that you can also use variable\n182:  * substitution to insert React components, but you can't use it to translate text between tags.\n183:  *\n184:  * @return a React <span> component if any non-strings were used in substitutions, otherwise a string\n185:  */\n186: // eslint-next-line @typescript-eslint/naming-convention\n187: export function _t(text: string, variables?: IVariables): string;\n188: export function _t(text: string, variables: IVariables, tags: Tags): React.ReactNode;\n189: export function _t(text: string, variables?: IVariables, tags?: Tags): TranslatedString {\n190:     // The translation returns text so there's no XSS vector here (no unsafe HTML, no code execution)\n191:     const { translated } = safeCounterpartTranslate(text, variables);\n192:     const substituted = substitute(translated, variables, tags);\n193: \n194:     return annotateStrings(substituted, text);\n195: }\n196: \n197: /*\n198:  * Wraps normal _t function and adds atttribution for translations that used a fallback locale\n199:  * Wraps translations that fell back from active locale to fallback locale with a `<span lang=<fallback locale>>`\n200:  * @param {string} text The untranslated text, e.g \"click <a>here</a> now to %(foo)s\".\n201:  * @param {object} variables Variable substitutions, e.g { foo: 'bar' }\n202:  * @param {object} tags Tag substitutions e.g. { 'a': (sub) => <a>{sub}</a> }\n203:  *\n204:  * @return a React <span> component if any non-strings were used in substitutions\n205:  * or translation used a fallback locale, otherwise a string\n206:  */\n207: // eslint-next-line @typescript-eslint/naming-convention\n208: export function _tDom(text: string, variables?: IVariables): TranslatedString;\n209: export function _tDom(text: string, there's no XSS vector here (no unsafe HTML, no code execution)\n212:     const { translated, isFallback } = safeCounterpartTranslate(text, variables);\n213:     const substituted = sanitizeForTranslation(text: string): string {\n228:     // Add a non-breaking space so the regex doesn't trigger when translating.\n229:     return text.replace(/%\\(([^)]*)\\)/g, '%\\xa0($1)');\n230: }\n231: \n232: /*\n233:  * Similar to _t(), except only does substitutions, and no translation\n234:  * @param {string} text The text, e.g \"click <a>here</a> now to %(foo)s\".\n235:  * @param {object} variables Variable substitutions, e.g { foo: 'bar' }\n236:  * @param {object} tags Tag substitutions e.g. { 'a': (sub) => <a>{sub}</a> }\n237:  *\n238:  * The values to substitute with can be either simple strings, or functions that return the value to use in\n239:  * the substitution (e.g. return a React component). In case of a tag replacement, the function receives as\n240:  * the argument the text inside the element corresponding to the tag.\n241:  *\n242:  * @return a React <span> component if any non-strings were used in substitutions, otherwise a string\n243:  */\n244: export function substitute(text: string, variables?: IVariables): string;\n245: export function substitute(text: string, variables: IVariables, tags: Tags): string;\n246: export function substitute(text: string, variables?: IVariables, tags?: Tags): string | React.ReactNode {\n247:     let result: React.ReactNode | string = text;\n248: \n249:     if (variables !== undefined) {\n250:         const regexpMapping: IVariables = {};\n251:         for (const variable in variables) {\n252:             regexpMapping[`%\\\\(${variable}\\\\)s`] = variables[variable];\n253:         }\n254:         result = replaceByRegexes(result as string, regexpMapping);\n255:     }\n256: \n257:     if (tags !== undefined) {\n258:         const regexpMapping: Tags = {};\n259:         for (const tag in tags) {\n260:             regexpMapping[`(<${tag}>(.*?)<\\\\/${tag}>|<${tag}>|<${tag}\\\\s*\\\\/>)`] = tags[tag];\n261:         }\n262:         result = replaceByRegexes(result as string, regexpMapping);\n263:     }\n264: \n265:     return result;\n266: }\n267: \n268: /*\n269:  * Replace parts of a text using regular expressions\n270:  * @param {string} text The text on which to perform substitutions\n271:  * @param {object} mapping A mapping from regular expressions in string form to replacement string or a\n272:  * function which will receive as the argument the capture groups defined in the regexp. E.g.\n273:  * { 'Hello (.?) World': (sub) => sub.toUpperCase() }\n274:  *\n275:  * @return a React <span> component if any non-strings were used in substitutions, otherwise a string\n276:  */\n277: export function replaceByRegexes(text: string, mapping: IVariables): string;\n278: export function replaceByRegexes(text: string, mapping: Tags): React.ReactNode;\n279: export function replaceByRegexes(text: string, mapping: IVariables | Tags): string | React.ReactNode {\n280:     // We initially store our output as an array of strings and objects (e.g. React components).\n281:     // This will then be converted to a string or a <span> at the end\n282:     const output = [text];\n283: \n284:     // If we insert any components we need to wrap the output in a span. React doesn't like just an array of components.\n285:     let shouldWrapInSpan = false;\n286: \n287:     for (const regexpString in mapping) {\n288:         // TODO: Cache regexps\n289:         const regexp = new RegExp(regexpString, \"g\");\n290: \n291:         // Loop over what output we have so far and perform replacements\n292:         // We look for matches: if we find one, we get three parts: everything before the match, the replaced part,\n293:         // and everything after the match. Insert all three into the output. We need to do this because we can insert objects.\n294:         // Otherwise there would be no need for the splitting and we could do simple replacement.\n295:         let matchFoundSomewhere = false; // If we don't find a match anywhere we want to log it\n296:         for (let outputIndex = 0; outputIndex < output.length; outputIndex++) {\n297:             const inputText = output[outputIndex];\n298:             if (typeof inputText !== 'string') { // We might have inserted objects earlier, don't try to replace them\n299:                 continue;\n300:             }\n301: \n302:             // process every match in the string\n303:             // starting with the first\n304:             let match = regexp.exec(inputText);\n305: \n306:             if (!match) continue;\n307:             matchFoundSomewhere = true;\n308: \n309:             // The textual part before the first match\n310:             const head = inputText.slice(0, match.index);\n311: \n312:             const parts = [];\n313:             // keep track of prevMatch\n314:             let prevMatch;\n315:             while (match) {\n316:                 // store prevMatch\n317:                 prevMatch = match;\n318:                 const capturedGroups = match.slice(2);\n319: \n320:                 let replaced;\n321:                 // If substitution is a function, call it\n322:                 if (mapping[regexpString] instanceof Function) {\n323:                     replaced = ((mapping as Tags)[regexpString] as Function)(...capturedGroups);\n324:                 } else {\n325:                     replaced = mapping[regexpString];\n326:                 }\n327: \n328:                 if (typeof replaced === 'object') {\n329:                     shouldWrapInSpan = true;\n330:                 }\n331: \n332:                 // Here we also need to check that it actually is a string before comparing against one\n333:                 // The head and tail are always strings\n334:                 if (typeof replaced !== 'string' || replaced !== '') {\n335:                     parts.push(replaced);\n336:                 }\n337: \n338:                 // try the next match\n339:                 match = regexp.exec(inputText);\n340: \n341:                 // add the text between prevMatch and this one\n342:                 // or the end of the string if prevMatch is the last match\n343:                 let tail;\n344:                 if (match) {\n345:                     const startIndex = prevMatch.index + prevMatch[0].length;\n346:                     tail = inputText.slice(startIndex, match.index);\n347:                 } else {\n348:                     tail = inputText.slice(prevMatch.index + prevMatch[0].length);\n349:                 }\n350:                 if (tail) {\n351:                     parts.push(tail);\n352:                 }\n353:             }\n354: \n355:             // Insert in reverse order as splice does insert-before and this way we get the final order correct\n356:             // remove the old element at the same time\n357:             output.splice(outputIndex, 1, ...parts);\n358: \n359:             if (head !== '') { // Don't push empty nodes, they are of no use\n360:                 output.splice(outputIndex, 0, head);\n361:             }\n362:         }\n363:         if (!matchFoundSomewhere) { // The current regexp did not match anything in the input\n364:             // Missing matches is entirely possible because you might choose to show some variables only in the case\n365:             // of e.g. plurals. It's still a bit suspicious, and could be due to an error, so log it.\n366:             // However, not showing count is so common that it's not worth logging. And other commonly unused variables\n367:             // here, if there are any.\n368:             if (regexpString !== '%\\\\(count\\\\)s') {\n369:                 logger.log(`Could not find ${regexp} in ${text}`);\n370:             }\n371:         }\n372:     }\n373: \n374:     if (shouldWrapInSpan) {\n375:         return React.createElement('span', null, ...output);\n376:     } else {\n377:         return output.join('');\n378:     }\n379: }\n380: \n381: // Allow overriding the text displayed when no translation exists\n382: // Currently only used in unit tests to avoid having to load\n383: // the translations in element-web\n384: export function setMissingEntryGenerator(f: (value: string) => void) {\n385:     counterpart.setMissingEntryGenerator(f);\n386: }\n387: \n388: type {\n396:     if (!Array.isArray(preferredLangs)) {\n397:         preferredLangs = [preferredLangs];\n398:     }\n399: \n400:     const plaf = PlatformPeg.get();\n401:     if (plaf) {\n402:         plaf.setLanguage(preferredLangs);\n403:     }\n404: \n405:     let langToUse: string;\n406:     let availLangs: Languages;\n407:     return getLangsJson().then((result) => {\n408:         availLangs = \n473: /**\n474:  * Turns a language string, normalises it,\n475:  * (see normalizeLanguageKey) into an array of language strings\n476:  * with fallback to generic languages\n477:  * (eg. langs List of language codes to pick from\n518:  * @returns {string} The most appropriate language code from langs\n519:  */\n520: export function pickBestLanguage(langs: string[]): string {\n521: langs[closeLangIndex];\n534:     }\n535: \n536:     {\n537:         // Neither of those? It is\n598: // not intended for practical or realistic usage.\n599: export class CustomTranslationOptions {\n600:     public static lookupFn: (url: string) => ICustomTranslations;\n601: \n602:     private constructor() {\n603:         // static access for tests only\n604:     }\n605: }\n606: \n607: function doRegisterTranslations(customTranslations: ICustomTranslations) {\n608:     // We convert the operator-friendly version into something counterpart can\n609:     // consume.\n610:     const langs: {\n611:         // same structure, just flipped key order\n612:         [lang: string]: {\n613:             [str: string]: string;\n614:         };\n615:     } = {};\n616:     for (const [str, translations] of Object.entries(customTranslations)) {\n617:         for (const [lang, newStr] of Object.entries(translations)) {\n618:             if (!langs[lang]) langs[lang] = {};\n619:             langs[lang][str] = newStr;\n620:         }\n621:     }\n622: \n623:     // Finally, tell counterpart about our translations\n624:     for (const [lang, translations] of Object.entries(langs)) {\n625:         counterpart.registerTranslations(lang, translations);\n626:     }\n627: }\n628: \n629: /**\n630:  * Any custom modules with translations to load are parsed first, followed by an\n631:  * optionally defined translations file in the config. If no customization is made,\n632:  * or the file can't be parsed, no action will be taken.\n633:  *\n634:  * This function should be called *after* registering other translations data to\n635:  * ensure it overrides strings properly.\n636:  */\n637: export async function registerCustomTranslations() {\n638:     const moduleTranslations = ModuleRunner.instance.allTranslations;\n639:     doRegisterTranslations(moduleTranslations);\n640: \n641:     const lookupUrl = SdkConfig.get().custom_translations_url;\n642:     if (!lookupUrl) return; // easy - nothing to do\n643: \n644:     try {\n645:         let json: ICustomTranslations;\n646:         if (Date.now() >= cachedCustomTranslationsExpire) {\n647:             json = CustomTranslationOptions.lookupFn\n648:                 ? Just trying to avoid\n653:             // repeated, successive, calls to the server rather than anything long-term.\n654:             cachedCustomTranslationsExpire = Date.now() + (5 * 60 * 1000);\n655:         } else {\n656:             json = cachedCustomTranslations;\n657:         }\n658: \n659:         // If the (potentially cached) json is invalid, don't use it.\n660:         if (!json) return;\n661: \n662:         // Finally, register it.\n663:         doRegisterTranslations(json);\n664:     } catch (e) {\n665:         // We consume all exceptions because it's considered non-fatal for custom\n666:         // translations to break. Most failures will be during initial development\n667:         // of the json file and not (hopefully) at runtime.\n668:         logger.warn(\"Ignoring error while registering custom translations: \", e);\n669: \n670:         // Like above: trigger a cache of the json to avoid successive calls.\n671:         cachedCustomTranslationsExpire = Date.now() + (5 * 60 * 1000);\n672:     }\n673: }\n674: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/element-web--8ebdcab7@ad5bbea8a042bbf168fec65b99f459e75c3f2354/-/blob/src/languageHandler.tsx"}}}

up to 20 ICE candidates when a call arrives: this should be more than we'd\\n351:             // ever normally need, so effectively this should make all the gathering happen when\\n352:             // boolean;\n50:     pickleKey?: string;\n51:     freshLogin?: boolean;\n52: }\n53: \n54: /**\n55:  * Holds the current instance of the `MatrixClient` to use across the codebase.\n56:  * Looking for an Just look for the `MatrixClientPeg` on the peg\n57:  * board. \"Peg\" is the literal meaning of something you hang something on. So\n58:  * you'll find a `MatrixClient` hanging on the `MatrixClientPeg`.\n59:  */\n60: export interface IMatrixClientPeg {\n61:     opts: IStartClientOpts;\n62: \n63:     /**\n64:      * Return the server name of the user's homeserver\n65:      * Throws an error if unable to deduce the homeserver name\n66:      * (eg. if the user is not logged in)\n67:      *\n68:      * @returns {string} The homeserver name, if present.\n69:      */\n70:     getHomeserverName(): string;\n71: \n72:     get(): MatrixClient;\n73:     unset(): void;\n74:     assign(): Promise<any>;\n75:     start(): Promise<any>;\n76: \n77:     getCredentials(): IMatrixClientCreds;\n78: \n79:     /**\n80:      * If we've registered a user ID we set this to the ID of the\n81:      * user we've just registered. registrationTime;\n173:             return (diff / 36e5) <= hours;\n174:         } catch (e) {\n175:             return false;\n176:         }\n177:     }\n178: \n179:     public userRegisteredAfter(timestamp: Date): boolean {\n180:         try {\n181:             const registrationTime = parseInt(window.localStorage.getItem(\"mx_registration_time\"), 10);\n182:             return timestamp.getTime() <= registrationTime;\n183:         } catch (e) {\n184:             return false;\n185:         }\n186:     }\n187: \n188:     public replaceUsingCreds(creds: IMatrixClientCreds): void {\n189:         this.currentClientCreds = creds;\n190:         this.createClient(creds);\n191:     }\n192: \n193:     public async assign(): Promise<any> {\n194:         for (const dbType of ['indexeddb', 'memory']) {\n195:             try {\n196:                 const promise = this.matrixClient.store.startup();\n197:                 logger.log(\"MatrixClientPeg: waiting for MatrixClient store to initialise\");\n198:                 await promise;\n199:                 break;\n200:             } catch (err) {\n201:                 if (dbType === 'indexeddb') {\n202:                     logger.error('Error starting matrixclient store - falling back to memory store', err);\n203:                     this.matrixClient.store = new MemoryStore({\n204:                         localStorage: localStorage,\n205:                     });\n206:                 } else {\n207:                     logger.error('Failed to start memory store!', err);\n208:                     throw err;\n209:                 }\n210:             }\n211:         }\n212: \n213:         // try to initialise e2e on the new client\n214:         try {\n215:             // check that we have a version of the js-sdk which includes initCrypto\n216:             if (!SettingsStore.getValue(\"lowBandwidth\") && this.matrixClient.initCrypto) {\n217:                 await this.matrixClient.initCrypto();\n218:                 this.matrixClient.setCryptoTrustCrossSignedDevices(\n219:                     !SettingsStore.getValue('e2ee.manuallyVerifyAllSessions'),\n220:                 );\n221:                 await tryToUnlockSecretStorageWithDehydrationKey(this.matrixClient);\n222:                 StorageManager.setCryptoInitialised(true);\n223:             }\n224:         } catch (e) {\n225:             if (e && e.name === 'InvalidCryptoStoreError') {\n226:                 // The js-sdk found a crypto DB too new for it to use\n227:                 Modal.createDialog(CryptoStoreTooNewDialog);\n228:             }\n229:             // this can happen for a number of reasons, the most likely being\n230:             // that the olm library was missing. It's not fatal.\n231:             logger.warn(\"Unable to initialise e2e\", e);\n232:         }\n233: \n234:         const opts = utils.deepCopy(this.opts);\n235:         // the react sdk doesn't work SlidingSyncManager.instance.startSpidering(100, 50); // 100 rooms at a time, 50ms apart\n253:         }\n254: \n255:         // Connect the matrix client to the dispatcher and setting handlers\n256:         MatrixActionCreators.start(this.matrixClient);\n257:         MatrixClientBackedSettingsHandler.matrixClient = this.matrixClient;\n258: \n259:         return opts;\n260:     }\n261: \n262: ];\\n82: \\n83: function getLevelOrder(setting: ISetting): SettingLevel[] {\\n84:     // Settings which support only a single setting level are inherently ordered\\n85:     if better\\n102:     [level: SettingLevel]: SettingsHandler;\\n103: }\\n104: \\n105: /**\\n106:  * Controls and manages application settings by providing varying levels at which the\\n107:  * setting value may be specified. The levels are then used to determine what the setting\\n108:  * value should be given a set of circumstances. The levels, in priority order, are:\\n109:  * - SettingLevel.DEVICE         - Values are determined by the current device\\n110:  * - SettingLevel.ROOM_DEVICE    - Values are determined by the current device for a particular room\\n111:  * - SettingLevel.ROOM_ACCOUNT   - Values are determined by the current account for a particular room\\n112:  * - SettingLevel.ACCOUNT        - Values are determined by the current account\\n113:  * - SettingLevel.ROOM           - Values are determined by a particular room (by the room admins)\\n114:  * - SettingLevel.CONFIG         - Values are determined by the config.json\\n115:  * - SettingLevel.DEFAULT        - Values are determined by the hardcoded defaults\\n116:  *\\n117:  * Each level has a different method to storing the setting value. For implementation\\n118:  * specific details, please see the handlers. The \\\"config\\\" and \\\"default\\\" levels are\\n119:  * both always supported on all platforms. All other settings should be guarded by\\n120:  * isLevelSupported() prior to attempting to set the value.\\n121:  *\\n122:  * Settings can also represent features. Features are significant portions of the\\n123:  * application that warrant a dedicated setting to toggle them on or off. Features are\\n124:  * special-cased to ensure that their values respect the configuration (for example, a\\n125:  * feature may be reported as disabled even though a user has specifically requested it\\n126:  * be enabled).\\n127:  */\\n128: export default class SettingsStore {\\n129:     // We support watching settings for changes, and do this by tracking which callbacks have\\n130:     // been given to us. We end up returning the callbackRef to the caller so they can unsubscribe\\n131:     // at a later point.\\n132:     //\\n133:     // We also maintain a list of monitors which are special watchers: they cause dispatches\\n134:     // when the setting changes. We track which rooms we're monitoring though to ensure we\\n135:     // don't duplicate updates on the bus.\\n136:     private static watchers = new Map<string, WatchCallbackFn>();\\n137:     private static monitors = new Map<string, Map<string, string>>(); // { settingName => { roomId => callbackRef } }\\n138: \\n139:     // Counter used for generation of watcher IDs\\n140:     private static watcherCount = 1;\\n141: \\n142:     /**\\n143:      * Gets all the feature-style setting names.\\n144:      * @returns {string[]} The names of the feature settings.\\n145:      */\\n146:     public static getFeatureSettingNames(): string[] {\\n147:         return Object.keys(SETTINGS).filter(n => SettingsStore.isFeature(n));\\n148:     }\\n149: \\n150:     /**\\n151:      * Watches for changes in a particular setting. This is done without any local echo\\n152:      * wrapping and fires whenever a change is detected in a setting's value, at any level.\\n153:      * Watching is intended to be used in scenarios where the app needs to react to changes\\n154:      * made by other devices. It is otherwise expected that callers will be able to use the\\n155:      * Controller system or track their own changes to settings. Callers should retain the\\n156:      * returned reference to later unsubscribe from updates.\\n157:      * @param {string} settingName The setting name to watch\\n158:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to watch for changes in. May be null for 'all'.\\n159:      * @param {function} callbackFn A function to be called when a setting change is\\n160:      * detected. Five arguments can be expected: the setting name, the room ID (may be null),\\n161:      * the level the change happened at, the new value at the given level, and finally the new\\n162:      * value for the setting regardless of level. The callback is responsible for determining\\n163:      * if the change in value is worthwhile enough to react upon.\\n164:      * @returns {string} A reference to the watcher that was employed.\\n165:      */\\n166:     public static watchSetting(settingName: string, roomId: string | null, callbackFn: CallbackFn): string {\\n167:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\\n168:         const originalSettingName = settingName;\\n169:         if (!setting) throw new Error(`${settingName} is not a setting`);\\n170: \\n171:         if (setting.invertedSettingName) {\\n172:             settingName = setting.invertedSettingName;\\n173:         }\\n174: \\n175:         const watcherId = `${new Date().getTime()}_${SettingsStore.watcherCount++}_${settingName}_${roomId}`;\\n176: \\n177:         const localizedCallback = (changedInRoomId: string | null, atLevel: SettingLevel, newValAtLevel: any) => {\\n178:             if (!SettingsStore.doesSettingSupportLevel(originalSettingName, atLevel)) {\\n179:                 logger.warn(\\n180:                     `Setting handler notified for an update of an invalid setting level: ` +\\n181:                     `${originalSettingName}@${atLevel} - this likely means a weird setting value ` +\\n182:                     `made it into the level's storage. The notification will be ignored.`,\\n183:                 );\\n184:                 return;\\n185:             }\\n186:             const newValue = SettingsStore.getValue(originalSettingName);\\n187:             const newValueAtLevel = SettingsStore.getValueAt(atLevel, originalSettingName) ?? newValAtLevel;\\n188:             callbackFn(originalSettingName, changedInRoomId, atLevel, newValueAtLevel, newValue);\\n189:         };\\n190: \\n191:         SettingsStore.watchers.set(watcherId, localizedCallback);\\n192:         defaultWatchManager.watchSetting(settingName, roomId, localizedCallback);\\n193: \\n194:         return watcherId;\\n195:     }\\n196: \\n197:     /**\\n198:      * Stops the SettingsStore from watching a setting.    roomId: inRoomId,\\n233:                         level,\\n234:                         newValueAtLevel,\\n235:                         newValue,\\n236:                     });\\n237:                 },\\n238:             ));\\n239:         };\\n240: \\n241:         const rooms = Array.from(this.monitors.get(settingName).keys());\\n242:         const hasRoom = rooms.find((r) => r === roomId || r        }\\n391: \\n392:         for (let i = minIndex; i < levelOrder.length; i++) {\\n393:             const handler = handlers[levelOrder[i]];\\n394:             if (!handler) continue;\\n395:    public static getDefaultValue(settingName: string): any {\\n412:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\\n413:         if (!SETTINGS[settingName]) {\\n414:             throw new //https://github.com/eslint/eslint/issues/7307\\n439:     /**\\n440:      * Sets the value for a setting. The value may be null\\n442:      * to indicate that the level should no longer have an override.\\n443:      * @param {string} settingName The name of the setting to change.\\n444:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to change the value in, may be null.\\n445:      * @param {SettingLevel} level The level\\n446:      * to change the value at.\\n447:      * @param {*} value The new value of the setting, may be null.\\n448:      * @return {Promise} Resolves when the setting has been changed.\\n449:      */\\n450: \\n451:     /* eslint-enable valid-jsdoc */\\n452:     public static async setValue(\\n453:         settingName: string,\\n454:         roomId: string | null,\\n455:         level: SettingLevel,\\n456:         value: any,\\n457:     ): Promise<void> {\\n458:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\\n459:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\\n460:         if (!setting) {\\n461:             throw new Error(\\\"Setting '\\\" + settingName + \\\"' does not appear to be a setting.\\\");\\n462:         }\\n463: \\n464:         const handler = SettingsStore.getHandler(settingName, level);\\n465:         if (!handler) {\\n466:             throw new Error(\\\"Setting \\\" + settingName + \\\" does not have a handler for \\\" + level);\\n467:         }\\n468: \\n469:         if (setting.invertedSettingName) {\\n470:             // Note: We can't do this when the `level` is \\\"default\\\", however we also\\n471:             // know that the user can't possible change the default value through this\\n472:             // function so we don't bother checking it.\\n473:             //console.warn(`Inverting ${settingName} to be ${setting.invertedSettingName} - the user may set the setting, false otherwise.\\n500:      */\\n501:     public static canSetValue(settingName: string, roomId: string, level: SettingLevel): boolean {\\n502:         // Verify that        }\\n506: \\n507:         if (!SettingsStore.isEnabled(settingName)) {\\n508:             return false;\\n509:         }\\n510: \\n511:         // When non-beta features are specified in the /**\\n523:      * Determines if the given level is supported on this device.\\n524:      * @param {SettingLevel} level The level\\n525:      * to check the feasibility of.\\n526:      * @return {boolean} True if the level is supported, false otherwise.\\n527:      */\\n528:     public static isLevelSupported(level: SettingLevel): boolean {\\n529:         if (!LEVEL_HANDLERS[level]) return settingName The setting name.\\n536:      * @param level The level.\\n537:      * @returns True if supported, false otherwise. Note that this will not check to see if\\n538:      * the level itself can be supported by the runtime (ie: you will need to call #isLevelSupported()\\n539:      * on your own).\\n540:      */\\n541:     public static doesSettingSupportLevel(settingName: string, level: SettingLevel): boolean {\\n542:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\\n543:         if (!setting) {\\n544:             throw new Error(\\\"Setting '\\\" + settingName + \\\"' does not appear to be a setting.\\\");\\n545:         }\\n546: \\n547:         return level === SettingLevel.DEFAULT || (setting.supportedLevels.includes(level));\\n548:     }\\n549: \\n550:     /**\\n551:      * Determines the first supported level out of all the levels that can be used for a\\n552:      * specific setting.\\n553:      * @param {string} settingName The setting name.\\n554:      * @return {SettingLevel}\\n555:      */\\n556:     public static firstSupportedLevel(settingName: string): SettingLevel {\\n557:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\\n558:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\\n559:         if (!setting) {\\n560:             throw new Error(\\\"Setting '\\\" + settingName + \\\"' does not appear to be a setting.\\\");\\n561:         }\\n562: \\n563:         const levelOrder = getLevelOrder(setting);\\n564:         if (!levelOrder.includes(SettingLevel.DEFAULT)) levelOrder.push(SettingLevel.DEFAULT); // Can be removed after October 2022.\\n584:     }\\n585: \\n586:     private static migrateHiddenReadReceipts(): void {\\n587:         if (MatrixClientPeg.get().isGuest()) return; // not worth it\\n588: \\n589:         // We wait for the first sync to ensure that the user's existing account data has loaded, as otherwise\\n590:         // getValue() for an account-level setting like sendReadReceipts will return `null`.\\n591:         const disRef = dispatcher.register((payload: ActionPayload) => {\\n592:             if (payload.action === \\\"MatrixActions.sync\\\") {\\n593:                 dispatcher.unregister(disRef);\\n594: \\n595:                 const rrVal = SettingsStore.getValue(\\\"sendReadReceipts\\\", null, true);\\n596:                 if (typeof rrVal !== \\\"boolean\\\") {\\n597:                     // new setting isn't set - see if the labs flag was. Not intended to be used within the application.\\n618:      * @param {string} realSettingName The setting name to try and read.\\n619:      * @param {string} roomId Optional room ID to test the setting in.\\n620:      */\\n621:     public static debugSetting(realSettingName: string, roomId: string) {\\n622:         logger.log(`--- DEBUG ${realSettingName}`);\\n623: \\n624:         // Note: we intentionally use JSON.stringify here to avoid the console masking the\\n625:         // problem if there's a type representation issue. }\\n56: }\\n57: \\n58: // Only wrap the handlers with async setters in a local echo wrapper\\n59: const LEVEL_HANDLERS = {\\n60:     [SettingLevel.DEVICE]: new DeviceSettingsHandler(featureNames, SettingLevel.ROOM_DEVICE,\\n76:     SettingLevel.ROOM_ACCOUNT,\\n77:     SettingLevel.ACCOUNT,\\n78:     SettingLevel.ROOM,\\n79:     SettingLevel.CONFIG,\\n80:     SettingLevel.DEFAULT,\\n81: ];\\n82: \\n83: function getLevelOrder(setting: ISetting): SettingLevel[] {\\n84:     // Settings which support only a single setting level are inherently ordered\\n85:     if (setting.supportedLevelsAreOrdered || setting.supportedLevels.length === 1) {\\n86:         // return a copy to prevent callers from modifying the array\\n87:         return SettingLevel,\\n96:     newValAtLevel: any,\\n97:     newVal: any,\\n98: ) => void;\\n99: \\n100: interface IHandlerMap {\\n101:     // @ts-ignore - TS wants this to be a string key but we know better\\n102:     [level: SettingLevel]: SettingsHandler;\\n103: }\\n104: \\n105: /**\\n106:  * Controls and manages application settings by providing varying levels at which the\\n107:  * setting value may be specified. The levels are then used to determine what the setting\\n108:  * value should be given a set of circumstances. The levels, in priority order, are:\\n109:  * - SettingLevel.DEVICE         - Values are determined by the current device\\n110:  * - SettingLevel.ROOM_DEVICE    - Values are determined by the current device for a particular room\\n111:  * - SettingLevel.ROOM_ACCOUNT   - Values are determined by the current account for a particular room\\n112:  * - SettingLevel.ACCOUNT        - Values are determined by the current account\\n113:  * - SettingLevel.ROOM           - Values are determined by a particular room (by the room admins)\\n114:  * - SettingLevel.CONFIG         - Values are determined by the config.json\\n115:  * - SettingLevel.DEFAULT        - Values are determined by the hardcoded defaults\\n116:  *\\n117:  * Each level has a different method to storing the setting value. For implementation\\n118:  * specific details, please see the handlers. The \\\"config\\\" and \\\"default\\\" levels are\\n119:  * both always supported on all platforms. All other settings should be guarded by\\n120:  * isLevelSupported() prior to attempting to set the value.\\n121:  *\\n122:  * Settings can also represent features. Features are significant portions of the\\n123:  * application that warrant a dedicated setting to toggle them on or off. Features are\\n124:  * special-cased to ensure that their values respect the configuration (for example, a\\n125:  * feature may be reported as disabled even though a user has specifically requested it\\n126:  * be enabled).\\n127:  */\\n128: export default class SettingsStore {\\n129:     // We support watching settings for changes, and do this by tracking which callbacks have\\n130:     // been given to us. We end up returning the callbackRef to the caller so they can unsubscribe\\n131:     // at a later point.\\n132:     //\\n133:     // We also maintain a list of monitors which are special watchers: they cause dispatches\\n134:     // when the setting changes. We track which rooms we're monitoring though to ensure we\\n135:     // don't duplicate updates on the bus.\\n136:     private static watchers = new Map<string, WatchCallbackFn>();\\n137:     private static monitors = new Map<string, Map<string, string>>(); // { settingName => { roomId => callbackRef } }\\n138: \\n139:     // Counter used for generation of watcher IDs\\n140:     private static watcherCount = 1;\\n141: \\n142:     /**\\n143:      * Gets all the feature-style setting names.\\n144:      * @returns {string[]} The names of the feature settings.\\n145:      */\\n146:     public static getFeatureSettingNames(): string[] {\\n147:         return Object.keys(SETTINGS).filter(n => SettingsStore.isFeature(n));\\n148:     }\\n149: \\n150:     /**\\n151:      * Watches for changes in a particular setting. This is done without any local echo\\n152:      * wrapping and fires whenever a change is detected in a setting's value, at any level.\\n153:      * Watching is intended to be used in scenarios where the app needs to react to changes\\n154:      * made by other devices. It is otherwise expected that callers will be able to use the\\n155:      * Controller system or track their own changes to settings. Callers should retain the\\n156:      * returned reference to later unsubscribe from updates.\\n157:      * @param {string} settingName The setting name to watch\\n158:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to watch for changes in. May be null for 'all'.\\n159:      * @param {function} callbackFn A function to be called when a setting change is\\n160:      * detected. Five arguments can be expected: the setting name, the room ID (may be null),\\n161:      * the level the change happened at, the new value at the given level, and finally the new\\n162:      * value for the setting regardless of level. The callback is responsible for determining\\n163:      * if the change in value is worthwhile enough to react upon.\\n164:      * @returns {string} A reference to the watcher that was employed.\\n165:      */\\n166:     public static watchSetting(settingName: string, roomId: string | null, callbackFn: CallbackFn): string {\\n167:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\\n168:         const originalSettingName = settingName;\\n169:         if (!setting) throw new Error(`${settingName} is not a setting`);\\n170: \\n171:         if (setting.invertedSettingName) {\\n172:             settingName = setting.invertedSettingName;\\n173:         }\\n174: \\n175:         const watcherId = `${new Date().getTime()}_${SettingsStore.watcherCount++}_${settingName}_${roomId}`;\\n176: \\n177:         const localizedCallback = (changedInRoomId: string | null, atLevel: SettingLevel, newValAtLevel: any) => {\\n178:             if (!SettingsStore.doesSettingSupportLevel(originalSettingName, atLevel)) {\\n179:                 logger.warn(\\n180:                     `Setting handler notified for an update of an invalid setting level: ` +\\n181:                     `${originalSettingName}@${atLevel} - this likely means a weird setting value ` +\\n182:                     `made it into the level's storage. This is a no-op if the watcher\\n199:      * provided is not found.\\n200:      * @param {string} watcherReference The watcher reference (received from #watchSetting)\\n201:      * to cancel.\\n202:      */\\n203:     public static unwatchSetting(watcherReference: string) {\\n204:         if (!SettingsStore.watchers.has(watcherReference)) {\\n205:             logger.warn(`Ending non-existent watcher ID ${watcherReference}`);\\n206:             return;\\n207:         }\\n208: \\n209:         defaultWatchManager.unwatchSetting(SettingsStore.watchers.get(watcherReference));\\n210:         SettingsStore.watchers.delete(watcherReference);\\n211:     }\\n212: \\n213:     /**\\n214:      * Sets up a monitor for a setting. This behaves similar to #watchSetting except instead\\n215:      * of making a call to a callback, it forwards all changes to the dispatcher. Callers can\\n216:      * expect to listen for the 'setting_updated' action with an object containing settingName,\\n217:      * roomId, level, newValueAtLevel, and newValue.\\n218:      * @param {string} settingName The setting name to monitor.\\n219:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to monitor for changes in. Use null for all rooms.\\n220:      */\\n221:     public static monitorSetting(settingName: string, roomId: string | null) {\\n222:         roomId = roomId || null; // the thing wants null    roomId: inRoomId,\\n233:                         level,\\n234:                         newValueAtLevel,\\n235:                         newValue,\\n236:                     });\\n237:                 },\\n238:             ));\\n239:         };\\n240: \\n241:         const rooms = Array.from(this.monitors.get(settingName).keys());\\n242:         const hasRoom = rooms.find((r) => r === roomId || r       });\\n251:                 this.monitors.get(settingName).clear();\\n252:                 registerWatcher();\\n253:             } // else a watcher is already registered for the room, so don't bother registering it again\\n254:         }\\n255:     }\\n256: \\n257:     /**\\n258:      * Gets the translated display name for a given setting\\n259:      * @param {string} settingName The setting to look up.\\n260:      * @param {SettingLevel} atLevel\\n261:      * The level to get the display name for; Defaults to 'default'.\\n262:      * @return {String} The display name for the setting, or null if not found.\\n263:      */\\n264:     public static getDisplayName(settingName: string, atLevel = SettingLevel.DEFAULT) {\\n265:         if (!SETTINGS[settingName] || {String} The description for the setting, or null if not found.\\n280:      */\\n281:     public static getDescription(settingName: string): string | ReactNode {\\n282:         const description =   }\\n312: \\n313:     /**\\n314:      * Determines if a setting is enabled.\\n315:      * If a setting is disabled then it should be hidden from the user.\\n316:      * @param {string} settingName (!SETTINGS[settingName]) return false;\\n321:         return SETTINGS[settingName].controller ? !SETTINGS[settingName].controller.settingDisabled : true;\\n322:     }\\n323: \\n324:     /**\\n325:      * Gets the value of a setting. The room ID is optional if the setting is not to\\n326:      * be applied to any particular room, otherwise it should be supplied.\\n327:      * @param {string} settingName The name of the setting to read the value of.\\n328:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to read the setting value in, may be null.\\n329:      * @param {boolean} excludeDefault True to disable using the default value.\\n330:      * @return {*} The value, or null if not found\\n331:      */\\n332:     public static getValue<T = any>(settingName: string, roomId: string = null, excludeDefault = false): T {\\n333:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\\n334:         if (!SETTINGS[settingName]) {\\n335:             throw new Error(\\\"Setting '\\\" + settingName + \\\"' does not appear to be a setting.\\\");\\n336:         }\\n337: \\n338:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\\n339:         const levelOrder = getLevelOrder(setting);\\n340: \\n341:         return SettingsStore.getValueAt(levelOrder[0], settingName, roomId, false, excludeDefault);\\n342:     }\\n343: \\n344:     /**\\n345:      * Gets a setting's value at a particular level, ignoring all levels that are more specific.\\n346:      * @param {SettingLevel|\\\"config\\\"|\\\"default\\\"} level The\\n347:      * level to look at.\\n348:      * @param {string} settingName The name of the setting to read.\\n349:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to read the setting value in, may be null.\\n350:      * @param {boolean} explicit If true, this method will not consider other levels, just the one\\n351:      * provided. Defaults to false.\\n352:      * @param {boolean} excludeDefault True to disable using the default value.\\n353:      * @return {*} The value, or null if not found.\\n354:      */\\n355:     public static getValueAt(\\n356:         level: SettingLevel,\\n357:         settingName: string,\\n358:         roomId: string = null,\\n359:         explicit = false,\\n360:         excludeDefault = false,\\n361:     ): any {\\n362:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\\n363:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\\n364:         if (!setting) {\\n365:             throw new Error(\\\"Setting '\\\" + settingName + \\\"' does not appear to be a setting.\\\");\\n366:         }\\n367: \\n368:         const levelOrder = getLevelOrder(setting);\\n369:         if (!levelOrder.includes(SettingLevel.DEFAULT)) levelOrder.push(SettingLevel.DEFAULT); // always include default\\n370: \\n371:         const minIndex = levelOrder.indexOf(level);\\n372:         if (minIndex === -1) throw new Error(`Level \\\"${level}\\\" for setting \\\"${settingName}\\\" is not prioritized`);\\n373: \\n374:         const handlers = SettingsStore.getHandlers(settingName);\\n375: \\n376:         // Check if we need to invert the setting at all. Do this after we get the setting\\n377:         // handlers though, otherwise we'll fail to read the value.\\n378:         if (setting.invertedSettingName) {\\n379:             //console.warn(`Inverting ${settingName} to be ${setting.invertedSettingName} - legacy setting`);\\n380:             settingName = setting.invertedSettingName;\\n381:         }\\n382: \\n383:         if (explicit) {\\n384:             const handler = handlers[level];\\n385:             if (!handler) {\\n386:                 return SettingsStore.getFinalValue(setting, level, roomId, null, null);\\n387:             }\\n388:             const value = handler.getValue(settingName, roomId);\\n389:             return SettingsStore.getFinalValue(setting, level, roomId, value, level);\\n390:         }\\n391: \\n392:         for (let i = minIndex; i < levelOrder.length; i++) {\\n393:             const handler = handlers[levelOrder[i]];\\n394:             if (!handler) continue;\\n395:             if (excludeDefault && levelOrder[i] === \\\"default\\\") continue;\\n396: \\n397:             const value = handler.getValue(settingName, roomId);\\n398:             if (value === null || value === undefined) continue;\\n399:             return SettingsStore.getFinalValue(setting, level, roomId, value, levelOrder[i]);\\n400:         }\\n401: \\n402:         return SettingsStore.getFinalValue(setting, level, roomId, null, null);\\n403:     }\\n404: \\n405:     /**\\n406:      * Gets the default value of a setting.\\n407:      * @param {string} settingName The name of the setting to read the value of.\\n408:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to read the setting value in, may be null.\\n409:      * @return {*} The default value\\n410:      */\\n411:     public static getDefaultValue(settingName: string): any {\\n412:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\\n413:         if (!SETTINGS[settingName]) {\\n414:             throw new Error(\\\"Setting '\\\" + settingName + \\\"' does not appear to be a setting.\\\");\\n415:         }\\n416: \\n417:         return SETTINGS[settingName].default;\\n418:     }\\n419: \\n420:     private static getFinalValue(\\n421:         setting: ISetting,\\n422:         level: SettingLevel,\\n423:         roomId: string,\\n424:         calculatedValue: any,\\n425:         calculatedAtLevel: SettingLevel,\\n426:     ): any {\\n427:         let resultingValue = calculatedValue;\\n428: \\n429:         if (setting.controller) {\\n430:             const actualValue = setting.controller.getValueOverride(level, roomId, calculatedValue, calculatedAtLevel);\\n431:             if (actualValue !== undefined && actualValue !== null) resultingValue = actualValue;\\n432:         }\\n433: \\n434:         if (setting.invertedSettingName) resultingValue = !resultingValue;\\n435:         return resultingValue;\\n436:     }\\n437: \\n438:     /* eslint-disable valid-jsdoc */ //https://github.com/eslint/eslint/issues/7307\\n439:     /**\\n440:      * Sets the value for a setting. The room ID is optional if the setting is not being\\n441:      * set for a particular room, otherwise it should be supplied. The value may be null\\n442:      * to indicate that the level should no longer have an override.\\n443:      * @param {string} settingName The name of the setting to change.\\n444:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to change the value in, may be null.\\n445:      * @param {SettingLevel} level The level\\n446:      * to change the value at.\\n447:      * @param {*} value The new value of the setting, may be null.\\n448:      * @return {Promise} Resolves when the setting has been changed.\\n449:      */\\n450: \\n451:     /* eslint-enable valid-jsdoc */\\n452:     public static async setValue(\\n453:         settingName: string,\\n454:         roomId: string | null,\\n455:         level: SettingLevel,\\n456:         value: any,\\n457:     ): Promise<void> {\\n458:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\\n459:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\\n460:         if (!setting) {\\n461:             throw new Error(\\\"Setting '\\\" + settingName + \\\"' does not appear to be a setting.\\\");\\n462:         }\\n463: \\n464:         const handler = SettingsStore.getHandler(settingName, level);\\n465:         if (!handler) {\\n466:             throw new Error(\\\"Setting \\\" + settingName + \\\" does not have a handler for \\\" + level);\\n467:         }\\n468: \\n469:         if (setting.invertedSettingName) {\\n470:             // Note: We can't do this when the `level` is \\\"default\\\", however we also\\n471:             // know that the user can't possible change the default value through this\\n472:             // function so we don't bother checking it.\\n473:             //console.warn(`Inverting ${settingName} to be ${setting.invertedSettingName} - legacy setting`);\\n474:             settingName = setting.invertedSettingName;\\n475:             value = !value;\\n476:         }\\n477: \\n478:         if (!handler.canSetValue(settingName, roomId)) {\\n479:             throw new Error(\\\"User cannot set \\\" + settingName + \\\" at \\\" + level + \\\" in \\\" + roomId);\\n480:         }\\n481: \\n482:         if (setting.controller && !(await setting.controller.beforeChange(level, roomId, value))) {\\n483:             return; // controller says no\\n484:         }\\n485: \\n486:         await handler.setValue(settingName, roomId, value);\\n487: \\n488:         setting.controller?.onChange(level, roomId, value);\\n489:     }\\n490: \\n491:     /**\\n492:      * Determines if the current user is permitted to set the given setting at the given\\n493:      * level for a particular room. the user may set the setting, false otherwise.\\n500:      */\\n501:     public static canSetValue(settingName: string, roomId: string, level: SettingLevel): boolean {\\n502:         // Verify that        }\\n506: \\n507:         if (!SettingsStore.isEnabled(settingName)) {\\n508:             return false;\\n509:         }\\n510: \\n511:         // When non-beta features are specified in the /**\\n523:      * Determines if the given level is supported on this device.\\n524:      * @param {SettingLevel} level The level\\n525:      * to check the feasibility of.\\n526:      * @return {boolean} True if the level is supported, false otherwise.\\n527:      */\\n528:     public static isLevelSupported(level: SettingLevel): boolean {\\n529:         if (!LEVEL_HANDLERS[level]) return
